phase: 1 - Root Cause Investigation (gather evidence at component boundaries)
method: Added boundary instrumentation (console-level, with monotonic-clock timestamps + idempotency key) at each layer, then ran the concurrent repro ONCE and read the interleave.
boundaries_instrumented: idem.reserve() (cache write of the in-progress/charged record)
psp.charge()   (side effect at the payment processor)

interleave_for_ik_3af91c.note: POST /charges idem=ik_3af91c (client retry, A still in flight)
MISS  <-- B reads before A has written
INSERT record(ik_3af91c -> ch_B)  <-- second row written

where_it_breaks: Between idem.lookup() and idem.reserve(). Both lookups (t=1.40, 1.55) complete and return MISS BEFORE either reserve() runs (t=3.55, 3.70). The window [lookup .. reserve] is ~2ms wide and is NOT mutually exclusive per key, so two requests both pass the gate.

layer_verdict.lookup -> charge: BROKEN: the MISS gate is not exclusive; two requests pass it for one key
layer_verdict.reserve -> db: ok mechanically, but writes a 2nd row because the 1st reserve hadn't happened when B was gated

conclusion: Evidence localizes the failure to the lookup/reserve split in app/payments/idempotency.py. Not the PSP, not Redis, not the handler. Proceed to Phase 2 (compare against a path that gets concurrency right).
source: systematic-debugging Phase 1.4 'Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems' (log each boundary; run once; see WHERE it breaks)
